title: eriogonum giganteum var. compactum
intro: Eriogonum giganteum var. compactum, also known as the California Bush Poppy or Dwarf Woolly Mule's Ears, is a compact, shrubby perennial plant native to the deserts of California. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The plant has been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, offering various health benefits to those who use it. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water or oil.
